title: Extremely long username exceeding database field limit
expected_result: Request rejected before database insertion, no error stack trace exposed, generic error message returned

title: SQL injection payload in username field
expected_result: Input sanitized or rejected, authentication fails, no database error exposed, no unauthorized access granted

title: Unicode emoji characters in password field
expected_result: Password accepted if valid, properly encoded in storage, no encoding errors or truncation

title: Whitespace-only credentials submission
steps: Enter 50 tabs and spaces in password field
expected_result: Input trimmed or rejected, authentication fails, no session created, no bypass through whitespace manipulation

title: Concurrent parallel login requests with same credentials
steps: Submit login request in all tabs at same timestamp
Use valid credentials across all requests
Monitor server response and session creation
expected_result: All requests processed safely, only one active session created, no race conditions, no duplicate accounts or sessions

title: Null byte injection in username
expected_result: Null byte stripped or rejected, authentication fails, no string truncation vulnerability exploited

title: Extremely long password exceeding system limits
expected_result: Request rejected before processing, memory not exhausted, generic timeout or error returned, no DoS condition

title: Mixed encoding attack with double URL encoding
expected_result: Double encoding decoded safely, authentication fails for non-existent user, no bypass through encoding trickery

title: Empty string submission with CSRF bypass attempt
steps: Manually craft POST request with empty strings
expected_result: Request rejected, CSRF validation fails, no session created, no authentication bypass

title: Special characters that break JSON parsing in API
steps: Send raw API request with username: {"admin":true}
Submit login request directly to /api/login endpoint
expected_result: Input sanitized, JSON parsing errors handled gracefully, no server error exposed, authentication fails safely
